description: Choosing the utf8 charset alias silently drops four-byte characters on insert.
body: Failure mode: a column declared CHARACTER SET utf8 (the utf8mb3 alias) truncates or rejects four-byte code points, so emoji and rare CJK characters are lost on write. Detection signals: round-tripped emoji come back as a replacement character or the insert raises Incorrect string value, and SHOW CREATE TABLE reports utf8 not utf8mb4. Do not use utf8 for any user-facing text column. Safer fallback: define columns as utf8mb4 with a utf8mb4 collation, set the connection charset to match, and re-import affected rows from a clean source after the conversion.
